name: Basic Latin
description: The foundational ASCII character set including control characters, digits, uppercase and lowercase Latin letters, and basic punctuation.

name: Latin-1 Supplement
description: Extended Latin characters for Western European languages including accented vowels, ligatures, and additional symbols.

name: Latin Extended-A
description: Additional Latin characters for Central and Eastern European languages including Polish, Czech, Slovak, and Baltic alphabets.

name: Latin Extended-B
description: Further Latin extensions for African languages, phonetic transcriptions, and additional European language support.

name: IPA Extensions
description: Characters used in the International Phonetic Alphabet for representing the sounds of spoken language.

name: Spacing Modifier Letters
description: Spacing modifier letters used in phonetics and linguistic notation to mark phonetic features of adjacent letters.

name: Combining Diacritical Marks
description: Non-spacing combining marks including accents, diacritics, and other marks that attach to a preceding base character.

name: Greek and Coptic
description: Letters and symbols of the Greek and Coptic scripts, including classical and modern Greek alphabets.

name: Cyrillic
description: The Cyrillic script used for Russian, Ukrainian, Bulgarian, Serbian, and many other Slavic and non-Slavic languages.

name: Cyrillic Supplement
description: Additional Cyrillic characters for minority languages of the former Soviet Union not covered by the main Cyrillic block.

name: Hebrew
description: The Hebrew script used for writing Hebrew, Yiddish, Ladino, and other Jewish languages, written right-to-left.

name: Arabic
description: The Arabic script used for Arabic, Persian, Urdu, Pashto, and many other languages written right-to-left.

name: Syriac
description: The Syriac abjad script used for Classical Syriac and modern Neo-Aramaic dialects, written right-to-left.

name: Thaana
description: The Thaana script used for writing the Dhivehi (Maldivian) language, written right-to-left.

name: Devanagari
description: The Devanagari script used for Hindi, Sanskrit, Marathi, Nepali, and many other South Asian languages.

name: Bengali
description: The Bengali script (Bangla) used for writing Bengali, Assamese, and some other languages of eastern South Asia.

name: Gurmukhi
description: The Gurmukhi script used to write Punjabi, the official script of Punjab state in India.

name: Gujarati
description: The Gujarati script used for writing the Gujarati language spoken primarily in Gujarat state, India.

name: Tamil
description: The Tamil script, one of the oldest writing systems still in use, for the Tamil language of South India and Sri Lanka.

name: Telugu
description: The Telugu script for writing the Telugu language, primarily spoken in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, India.

name: Kannada
description: The Kannada script for writing the Kannada language, primarily spoken in Karnataka state, India.

name: Malayalam
description: The Malayalam script for writing the Malayalam language spoken in Kerala state, India.

name: Thai
description: The Thai script (Abugida) for writing the Thai language, including letters, vowel signs, tone marks, and digits.

name: Hangul Jamo
description: Individual Hangul consonants (jamo) and vowels used as building blocks for Korean syllable composition.

name: Ethiopic
description: The Ethiopic (Ge'ez) script used for Amharic, Tigrinya, Oromo, and other languages of Ethiopia and Eritrea.

name: Cherokee
description: The syllabary invented by Sequoyah for writing the Cherokee language, one of few indigenous North American scripts.

name: Khmer
description: The Khmer (Cambodian) script, an abugida derived from the ancient Brahmi script, used for the Khmer language.

name: Mongolian
description: The traditional Mongolian script written top-to-bottom, left-to-right, used in Inner Mongolia and historical texts.

name: Hiragana
description: The Japanese syllabary Hiragana, used for native Japanese words, grammatical elements, and as furigana readings.

name: Katakana
description: The Japanese syllabary Katakana, used primarily for foreign loanwords, technical terms, and onomatopoeia.

name: Bopomofo
description: The Zhuyin fuhao (Bopomofo) phonetic script used in Taiwan for annotating Mandarin Chinese pronunciation.

name: Hangul Compatibility Jamo
description: Hangul jamo characters in their standalone compatibility form, maintained for backward compatibility with older encodings.

name: CJK Unified Ideographs
description: The core block of unified Chinese, Japanese, and Korean (CJK) ideographic characters for modern use.

name: CJK Compatibility
description: CJK characters maintained for compatibility with legacy character sets; most are canonical equivalents of unified ideographs.

name: Hangul Syllables
description: All precomposed Hangul syllable blocks for the Korean writing system, algorithmically composable from Jamo.

name: High Surrogates
description: High surrogate code units used in UTF-16 encoding to represent supplementary characters above U+FFFF.

name: Low Surrogates
description: Low surrogate code units used in UTF-16 encoding as the second half of a surrogate pair for supplementary characters.

name: Private Use Area
description: Code points reserved for private agreements; commonly used for custom icons, emoji variants, and corporate symbols.

name: Letterlike Symbols
description: Symbols derived from letters, including trademark, copyright, registered sign, currency-like symbols, and mathematical scripts.

name: Number Forms
description: Vulgar fraction characters, Roman numerals, and other specialized numeric notation forms.

name: Arrows
description: Directional arrow symbols pointing in all directions, used in mathematics, logic, diagrams, and UI design.

name: Mathematical Operators
description: Mathematical and logical operator symbols including set notation, quantifiers, relations, and arithmetic operators.

name: Miscellaneous Technical
description: Technical symbols used in computing, telecommunications, and engineering including keyboard symbols and projective symbols.

name: Control Pictures
description: Visible representations of ASCII control characters (NUL, SOH, STX, etc.) for documentation and reference purposes.

name: Box Drawing
description: Line-drawing characters for constructing text-mode boxes, tables, and UI borders in terminal applications.

name: Block Elements
description: Block element characters for pixel-art style graphics in terminals, including half-blocks and quarter-blocks.

name: Geometric Shapes
description: Geometric shape symbols including squares, triangles, circles, diamonds, and other polygons in filled and outline forms.

name: Miscellaneous Symbols
description: General-purpose symbols including weather, celestial bodies, card suits, chess pieces, recycling, and many others.

name: Dingbats
description: Ornamental characters and typographic dingbats including checkmarks, crosses, stars, arrows, and decorative marks.

name: Supplemental Arrows-A
description: Additional arrow symbols supplementing the main Arrows block, including long arrows and arrow combining marks.

name: Supplemental Mathematical Operators
description: Additional mathematical operators not included in the main Mathematical Operators block for advanced mathematics.

name: Miscellaneous Symbols and Arrows
description: Additional symbols and arrows for use in various technical, mathematical, and general-purpose contexts.

name: CJK Radicals Supplement
description: Supplementary forms of the Kangxi radicals used in Chinese-Japanese-Korean dictionaries and ideograph indexing.

name: Kangxi Radicals
description: The 214 traditional Kangxi radicals used for indexing Chinese characters in dictionaries.

name: Latin Extended Additional
description: Additional Latin characters mainly for Vietnamese, Welsh, and other languages needing multiple diacritics on letters.

name: General Punctuation
description: General punctuation marks including various dashes, quotation marks, spaces of different widths, and typographic symbols.

name: Currency Symbols
description: Currency symbols for monetary systems worldwide, including Euro, Pound, Yen, Rupee, Ruble, Bitcoin, and others.

name: Enclosed Alphanumerics
description: Circled and enclosed numbers and letters, used in lists, annotations, and as decorative or functional markers.

name: Mathematical Alphanumeric Symbols
description: Mathematical alphabets in bold, italic, fraktur, script, double-struck, and other styles for typesetting mathematics.

name: Emoticons
description: Emoji faces and people symbols including smileys, emotions, gestures, and simple human figure expressions.

name: Miscellaneous Symbols and Pictographs
description: A wide range of emoji including nature, food, travel, activities, objects, and other pictographic symbols.

name: Transport and Map Symbols
description: Emoji symbols for vehicles, transportation modes, traffic signs, and map-related icons.

name: Supplemental Symbols and Pictographs
description: Additional emoji including supplemental faces, hand gestures, clothing, animals, and food introduced in newer Unicode versions.

name: Symbols and Pictographs Extended-A
description: Extended emoji pictographs for chess pieces, board game symbols, and additional miscellaneous symbols.
